email_draft.subject: Parallel development & remote dev environments
email_draft.body: Hi Kolya, I caught your recent post on using VSCode Remote SSH to manage independent dev containers. It’s a pragmatic solution for the bottleneck you described. I’m reaching out because [Company Name] is solving the exact scalability issues you’re tackling at Mercura regarding [Specific Tech/Infrastructure Alignment]. I’d love to share how we’re reducing overhead without compromising the 'Silicon Valley speed' you prioritize. Do you have 10 minutes to discuss how you're handling [Specific Technical Challenge]?

linkedin_inmail.body: Hi Kolya, loved your take on Git Worktrees vs. independent remote dev environments. As a fellow engineer, that balance between velocity and technical debt is a constant struggle. Would love to connect and share some insights on building AI infrastructure for scale.

high_resonance_topics: Scaling cloud infrastructure for parallel feature testing
Engineering architecture for industrial AI applications
topics_to_avoid: Abstract software theories without concrete implementation plans

conversation_starters.opener: That remote dev setup you detailed on LinkedIn—have you looked into [Specific Tooling] to handle those container migrations?
conversation_starters.context: Direct technical engagement based on his recent content.

conversation_starters.opener: Saw the photos from the Hochkönig offsite. How do you balance that high-intensity team bonding with your ship-velocity goals?
conversation_starters.context: Acknowledging his focus on both team culture and engineering throughput.

conversation_starters.opener: Mercura's focus on the AI application layer is fascinating. How are you managing the trade-off between AI-assisted speed and maintaining code quality/long-term debt?
conversation_starters.context: Demonstrating high-level technical empathy regarding his specific operational role.
